CREATE TABLE nums (a INT, b INT, c INT);
INSERT INTO nums VALUES (3, 2, 4);
SELECT least(a, b, c) FROM nums;
INSERT INTO nums VALUES (5, NULL, 1);
SELECT least(a, b, c) FROM nums;
CREATE TABLE more_nums (x INT, y INT);
INSERT INTO more_nums VALUES (2, 7);
SELECT least(mn.a, mn.b, m.y) FROM nums AS mn JOIN more_nums AS m ON mn.a = 3;
SELECT least(3, least(1, 2), 4);
SELECT least(LEAST(a, b), c) FROM nums;
SELECT least(ABS(-5), 5);
CREATE TABLE test_table (value1 INT, value2 INT);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (NULL, 1), (2, NULL), (NULL, NULL);
SELECT least(value1, value2) FROM test_table;
SELECT least(least(a, b), c) AS smallest_value FROM nums WHERE c IS NOT NULL;
SELECT least(ARRAY[10, 20, 5, 15]) AS smallest_array_value;
CREATE TABLE complex_test (id INT, val1 DOUBLE, val2 DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO complex_test VALUES (1, 1.5, 2.5), (2, 0.5, -1.0);
SELECT least(SELECT MIN(val1) FROM complex_test) AS smallest_val FROM complex_test;
